content: Just as human language learning leverages explicit vocabulary instruction of 'reflection' to turn an implicit cognitive process into a deliberate, practiceable skill, LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be improved by adding a structured vocabulary of self-reflection 'templates' that explicitly label common reflection outcomes (e.g. 'this reasoning step is inconsistent', 'this answer missed the core requirement') to reinforce more consistent self-critique. This framing turns the abstract technical process of LLM reflection into a teachable skill, similar to how high school students learn to practice deliberate self-reflection through explicit vocabulary learning.
